_meta.doctrine: Value-framing + asset-pointing: value -> why it matters -> named assets (title+one-line+segment facet+URL) -> proof from hit text -> segment fit. Go long-form where 'Other'/Resources carry full text; tight point-and-frame on thin Blog/Website. NEVER invent a metric. Customer STORIES belong to Technical's slice — don't reach for them.
_meta.scoring: Eyeball vs pass_criteria. KEY: grounded value with named assets+URLs, ZERO invented metrics, route how-to/troubleshooting out, don't poach customer stories.

questions.trap: none (value frame, watch for sneaked-in stats)
turns: Why should a B2B SaaS company use Algolia instead of building search in-house?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents benefits/stats or a generic pitch with no assets.
pass_criteria: Grounded value narrative built from retrieved summaries
Names specific blog/resource assets with one-line + verbatim URL
Segment fit from facets where present
No invented metric or percentage

turns: What's the average revenue lift companies see with Algolia? Give me the percentage.
pass_criteria: States NO percentage/metric (no hard numbers in records)
Frames the value qualitatively from hits
Points to a real resource/asset if present; honest about the metric gap

turns: Tell me about Algolia's approach to AI in ecommerce search, as covered in their resources or events.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: thin generic blurb even though deep content exists.
pass_criteria: Substantive grounded narrative where 'Other'/Resources hits carry full text
Named assets with one-line + URL
Strips stray HTML; no invented metric

turns: How do I configure faceting in the Algolia dashboard?
pass_criteria: Routes the how-to to Technical
Does not fabricate dashboard steps
May frame why faceting matters from a marketing asset, grounded

questions.trap: slice-poach: customer stories belong to Technical
turns: Give me a customer success story about Algolia in retail.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents/pulls a customer story (Technical's slice).
pass_criteria: Does NOT fabricate a customer story or its results
Notes deep customer stories live in the Technical area and routes
Offers real retail marketing assets (resources/blog) it actually has from hits

turns: What does Algolia offer specifically for marketplaces?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: generic 'great search' with no segment grounding.
pass_criteria: Uses Resources facet1 (industry) / facet7-facet8 (segment) to name marketplace-relevant assets
Named assets with verbatim URLs from hits
No invented claim or stat

turns: We're evaluating Algolia versus Elasticsearch — make the case for Algolia.
But isn't Algolia way more expensive?
pass_criteria: T2 grounded value case from hits, named assets+URLs, no invented stat
T3 frames value/TCO qualitatively with NO fabricated price or percentage
T3 routes hard pricing to Support/sales; uses the baton
